close_reason: Positive observation: two-stage validation is correct design
description:
lib.rs:117-170 splits algorithm validation from key parsing into two functions, with parse_key containing an unreachable!() arm for already-rejected algorithm strings.

This LOOKS redundant — the natural one-pass form is to match algo_str inside parse_key and return InvalidUri for unknown. The two-stage form is INTENTIONAL: it ensures unknown-algorithm URIs return InvalidUri WITHOUT performing any I/O on parsed.path(). The single-pass form would have to read the key file first (to have key_bytes for the match arms) and then return InvalidUri after the I/O — leaking timing info about file existence even when the algorithm is wrong, and wasting an std::fs::read.

The unreachable!() is well-commented (lib.rs:166-168) so anyone considering 'fixing' it will see why.

DEFEND. Do not consolidate.
title: DEFEND: Two-stage validate_algorithm + parse_key separation
